title: Jamaica Mi Hungry closes restaurant, shifts focus back to food truck
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Jamaica Mi Hungry closes its Downtown Boston restaurant, focusing on catering and food truck services instead.
The food truck will serve at Kendall Square, MIT, and Boston Common on specified weekdays throughout the summer.
Chef Ernie Campbell started Jamaica Mi Hungry in 2012; the brand has won multiple awards for its Jamaican cuisine.

title: Report finds former Canton Sgt. Sean Goode used ‘horrific’ language in texts
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Former Canton Sergeant Sean Goode used offensive language targeting various ethnic groups in messages, report by MJ Myers Consulting shows.
Goode was placed on leave in October before resigning; his police certification was suspended by Massachusetts POST Commission in June.
Report includes Goode's references to minorities, women, and disabled individuals; calls for permanent disqualification from police service.

title: Alvin Campbell Jr., serial rapist and AG’s brother, receives life in prison
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Alvin Campbell Jr., Attorney General Andrea Campbell's brother, was sentenced to life in prison for raping five women.
Campbell posed as an Uber driver to prey on women between 2017 and 2019, facing 21 convictions from a Suffolk County jury.
He also faces pending charges for additional assaults in jail, including attacking a correctional officer in 2025.

title: Officials identify man killed in wrong-way crash in Boston
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Riley Sergi, 28, from Haverhill, was killed in a wrong-way crash on I-93 in Boston.
Wrong-way driver Antone Carvalho, 81, from Somerset, will face court; charges pending ongoing investigation.
Crash involved multiple vehicles; family of four hospitalized, no updates provided on their condition.

title: Germany vs. Paraguay World Cup live coverage: Paraguay takes 1-0 lead
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paraguay leads 1-0 against Germany in the World Cup Round of 32 match at Boston Stadium in Foxborough.
The winner will face either France or Sweden in the Round of 16 on July 4 in Philadelphia.
Germany, Group E winners, struggled in group stage but secured advancement with crucial victories.

title: Revolution announce front office shift: Mass. native Chris Tierney named general manager
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Chris Tierney appointed as New England Revolution's general manager and chief soccer officer.
Curt Onalfo transitions to senior vice president of soccer operations, focusing on team development.
Both Tierney and Onalfo will report to club president Brian Bilello.

title: Man allegedly pepper sprays woman jogging in apparent random assault in Maine coastal city
author: John Waller
bullets: A 37-year-old man allegedly pepper sprayed a woman jogging in Rockland, Maine, on Saturday.
The suspect was chased off by witnesses and subsequently arrested by local police.
He faces multiple charges including assault, criminal threatening, and use of disabling chemicals.

title: Noah Kahan asks his audience to please not poop at venues (except in designated areas)
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Noah Kahan urges concertgoers to use restrooms after an incident at Citizens Bank Park, Philadelphia.
Kahan asks fans to stop stealing Alger Brook Road signs in Strafford, Vermont.
Kahan offers to pay for replacement signs and seeks solutions with town officials.

title: Hiker rescued by helicopter after 50-foot fall in Huntington Ravine
author: John Waller
bullets: A 24-year-old Quebec City man was rescued after a 50-foot fall on Mount Washington's Huntington Ravine Trail.
New Hampshire National Guard used a helicopter to airlift the hiker to Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center in Lebanon.
Officials warn that the Huntington Ravine Trail is unsuitable for casual hikers due to its dangerous cliff climbs.

title: State trooper relieved of duty after allegedly being captured on video knocking out other trooper in bar fight
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Massachusetts State Police trooper was relieved of duty after a bar fight with another trooper in Quincy.
Both involved troopers were off-duty at the ReelHouse Marina Bay when the altercation occurred.
The Department's Office of Professional Integrity is investigating, and the trooper remains suspended pending a formal hearing.
